summary: Iteration 40 - CTO Role Visible Tabs Bug Fix Verified. All 14 backend tests passed. The fix in get_visible_tabs() correctly converts custom role permissions to visible tabs.

bug_tested.description: After creating a new role CTO and a new employee for that role, when logging in with that new employee's credentials, no other screens are available for access other than dashboard.
bug_tested.root_cause: get_visible_tabs() in rbac.py was only checking TAB_VISIBILITY dictionary for preset roles. Custom roles like CTO with permissions stored in the database were not being processed.
bug_tested.fix_applied: Modified get_visible_tabs() to check if role has custom permissions stored. If so, convert those permissions to visible tabs using PAGE_TO_TAB mapping. Also added 'employees' -> 'hr' mapping.
evidence: /auth/me returns visible_tabs: [customers, inspections, hr, dashboard, leads, finance, settings]
All expected tabs present based on CTO role permissions
Dashboard is always included when user has any visible tabs
PAGE_TO_TAB mapping correctly converts 'employees' permission to 'hr' tab

critical_code_review_comments: Fix correctly handles custom roles with permissions stored in database
PAGE_TO_TAB mapping properly converts page names to tab names
Dashboard is always added when user has any visible tabs
Fallback to TAB_VISIBILITY for preset roles without custom permissions works correctly
context_for_next_testing_agent: CTO visible tabs bug is fixed. Custom roles with permissions stored in database now correctly show all tabs they have 'view' permission for. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_cto_visible_tabs.py
